seoTitle: Fitzroy Island vs Flametree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Fitzroy Island and Flametree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Fitzroy Island vs Flametree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fitzroy Island and Flametree.
heading: Fitzroy Island vs Flametree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fitzroy Island, Queensland and Flametree, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Flametree (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fitzroy Island or Flametree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Island has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Flametree scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fitzroy Island scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Flametree scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fitzroy Island or Flametree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fitzroy Island has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
